name: Build inside Maestro (house rule)
description: The non-negotiable way to work — build every edit ON the Maestro timeline via MCP tools so the user watches it happen, and render only with Maestro's own Export. Read before any editing task.

name: Viral-style reel
description: Turn raw footage into a punchy vertical (9:16) short: hook in the first 3s, tight jump cuts, zoom punches, trending captions, and a beat-aware rhythm. Use when asked to 'make this a viral reel / short / TikTok / Reel'.

name: Beat-synced cutting
description: Cut and animate to the music. Use analyze_audio to get beats/onsets, then place cuts and zoom/scale punches on the beat. Use when the edit should feel locked to a track.

name: Creative director (palette, grade, type)
description: Give a clip or project a cohesive look: extract its palette, build a color grade, and pick on-brand type + captions. Use for 'make it look cinematic / on-brand / designed'.

name: Trending caption styles
description: Apply modern short-form caption looks — word-pop, karaoke highlight, bold-boxed, typewriter — with correct sizing/placement for 9:16. Use when adding or restyling on-screen text/captions.

name: B-roll planner
description: Plan and place B-roll cutaways over an A-roll talking track — from the media library or generated (BYOK). Use when the edit needs visual variety or to illustrate what's being said.

name: Platform delivery
description: Package the finished edit for each platform — Reels/TikTok/Shorts (9:16), feed (1:1), YouTube (16:9) — with the right aspect, safe areas, duration, and captions, then export. Use when delivering for social/YouTube.

name: Promo / ad video
description: Build a short promo or ad — hook, benefit beats, and a clear call-to-action — with titles, captions, music, and pacing. Use for 'make a promo / ad / product video'.
